content.full_text:
CalifOrnia
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
August 2A, 1990
E. Jane PooI
city clerk
city of Encinitas
527 Encinitas Boulevard
Enc j-nitas, cA 92024
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-9 0-503
Dear lils. Pool:
This is in response to your request for advice regardinT the
contribution lirnits of the Political Reforn Act (the 'rActtr).
since your request is for general information and does not relate
to a specific decision, we are treating your request as one for
informil assistance.2
OUESTION
Are contri.butions of husbands and wives cumulated for
purposes of the Act's contribution limits?
CONCLUSION
The contributions of husbands and wives are not cumulated for
purposes of the contribution tinits. 
Each spouse may contribute
the maximun amount pernitted to be contributed by a person under
the Act.
1 co.r".rr."nt Code Sections 81OOO-91015. Al.L statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seo. A11 references to regulatj.ons are to TitLe ?
Division 6 of the california code of Regulations.
2 Infornal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
inrnunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114i 2 Cal . Code of Regs.
Section 18329 (c) (3) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo o P.o. Box 8oz o sacramento cA 958o4-ogoz . (916) 3 zz-s66o


Our F■■e No. ■-90-503
Page 2
FACTS
As city cIerk, you anticipate being asked during the November
carnpa j.gm whether contributions of husbands and wives are cumulated
for purposes of the campaign contribution linits of the Act. rn
1988, you were advised that contributions would be cumulated due
to California community property laws. You have read a recent
sunmary of an advice letter issued by the Commission (Grant Advice
Letter No. I-9o-144, copy enctosed) which seems to contradict your
understanding. You seek clarification 
of the commission's advj.ce
regarding cumulation of contributions of spouses for purposes of
the canpaign contribution lilrits.
ANALYSIS
The Act defines rrpersonrr for most purposes as fol'1o$rs:
rrPerson neans an individuaL, proprietorship,
firm, partnership, joint venture, syndi.cate,
business trust, company, corporation, association,
cornrnittee, and anv other orqanization or qroup of
Dersons actinq in concert.
Section 82047 (enphasis added).
Horrever, Proposition 73, a statewide initiative 
passed by the
voters at the June 1988 statewide prinary election, added a new
definition of rrpersonrt for purposes of the contribution limits
added to the Act by the initi.ative, 
(Tit1e 9, chaPter 5.) 
For
purposes of the contribution lirnits, rtpersonrr is defined as fol-
lows:
trPersonu means an individual, proprietorship,
finn, partnership, joint venture, syndicate,
business trust, company, corporation, assocj.ation,
committee, and labor organization.
Section 85102 (b).
Based upon the differences between the ts/o definitions, the
Commission has detennined that contributions of husbands and wives
should not be cumulated. Each spouse may therefore contribute the
naxirnum amount contributable by a person--gl,ooo to a candidate
(Section 85301) and $2,500 to a political 
committee to be used to
support or oppose candidates (Section 85302). (In re Bel1 (1988)
11 FPPC Ops 1; Clendenin Advice Letter No. I-99-624, copies
enclosed . )
The Grant advice letter, supra, reaffirms that husbands and
wives are treated as separate persons under the Act. However, the
Grant letter additionally discussed a proposed situation in which
a husband and wife forned a partnership. The issue was whether a
contribution by the partnership would iestrict 
the ability of


Our Fi■e No. 
■-90-503
´      Page 3
individual partners to make contributions to the same candidate.
The crant letter indicated that the contributions of the Dartner-
ship would be cumulated with a sPouse's individuat contribution
onty as to a spouse who in fact directs and controls the contribu-
tions of the psrlnelSghlp.
I trust that this has provided you with the information that
you have requested. If you have any questions, P1ease contact me
at (916) 322-5901.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
'- '.?'r7 
z/ /; i , { z' /z
-/tLa^-u,-,e l/. Y/-
tJ
By: Margaret w. Ellison
counsel, Legal Division
Enclosures
